title: Copy-Paste Errors (Tandem Duplication)
learning_goal: Learn about tandem duplication - a real mutational mechanism (often caused by DNA polymerase 'slipping' and re-reading the same stretch of template twice during replication) where a segment of DNA ends up copied immediately next to itself. Unlike Level 4's insertion, the extra letters here are NOT new/arbitrary - they are an exact copy of nearby sequence. Whether a duplication causes a frameshift depends on the same multiple-of-3 rule as insertions and deletions.
rules: You'll work with whichever gene you chose in Level 1 - your choice carries over automatically.
This level has 2 examples: an IN-FRAME duplication (one whole codon copied, 3 nt - no frameshift) and a FRAMESHIFT duplication (2 nt copied - not a multiple of 3, so it shifts the reading frame).
In both cases, the extra letters are not arbitrary - they're an exact copy of the sequence immediately before them. That's what makes this a 'duplication' rather than a generic insertion.
For the in-frame example, only ONE extra amino acid appears (a repeat of the duplicated codon's amino acid) - everything else in the protein stays identical.
For the frameshift example, everything from the duplication point onward is scrambled, exactly like Level 4's insertion - because 2 is not a multiple of 3.
materials_provided: The reference nucleotide sequence and full amino acid sequence, shown on screen the whole time
Your sample's full nucleotide sequence, with a tandem-duplicated segment inserted
A row of candidate duplication points (gaps between reference codons) for the player to test
instructions: 1. Compare your sample to the reference - it has extra letters that exactly match the sequence right before them.
2. Locate the gap where the duplication happened, the same way as Level 4.
3. For the in-frame example: compare the reference protein to your sample's protein directly - find the one extra repeated amino acid.
4. For the frameshift example: re-split the sequence into codons of 3 from position 1 and re-translate, exactly like Level 4 - notice everything downstream is now different.

duplication_details.classification: In-frame tandem duplication - codon 10 (GGA, Glycine) got copied right after itself. Because 3 IS a multiple of 3, the reading frame is NOT disturbed - the protein just gains one extra Glycine in a row, and everything else, including everything downstream, translates identically to the reference.

duplication_details.why_multiple_of_3_matters: This time only 2 nucleotides ('TT', copied from right before the insertion point) got tandem-duplicated - and 2 is NOT a multiple of 3, so unlike the in-frame example, this duplication shifts every downstream codon boundary, exactly like the insertion in Level 4 - a frameshift.

duplication_details.classification: In-frame tandem duplication - codon 10 (AGA, Arginine) got copied right after itself. Because 3 IS a multiple of 3, the reading frame is NOT disturbed - the protein just gains one extra Arginine in a row, and everything else, including everything downstream, translates identically to the reference.

duplication_details.why_multiple_of_3_matters: This time only 2 nucleotides ('AT', copied from right before the insertion point) got tandem-duplicated - and 2 is NOT a multiple of 3, so unlike the in-frame example, this duplication shifts every downstream codon boundary, exactly like the insertion in Level 4 - a frameshift.

duplication_details.classification: In-frame tandem duplication - codon 10 (TTG, Leucine) got copied right after itself. Because 3 IS a multiple of 3, the reading frame is NOT disturbed - the protein just gains one extra Leucine in a row, and everything else, including everything downstream, translates identically to the reference.

duplication_details.why_multiple_of_3_matters: This time only 2 nucleotides ('AA', copied from right before the insertion point) got tandem-duplicated - and 2 is NOT a multiple of 3, so unlike the in-frame example, this duplication shifts every downstream codon boundary, exactly like the insertion in Level 4 - a frameshift.
